MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-07-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two
Off Calendar Jul-19-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Donald E. Mahnke, Esq. Quill & Arrow LLP, 310-933-4271 dmahnke@quillarrowlaw.com for Plaintiff OSCAR ANTONIO MENDEZ ESQUIVEL
Not other appearances.

Not ARGUED.Plaintiff submits to the Court's tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF OSCAR ANTONIO MENDEZ ESQUIVEL's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two

The motion to compel is GRANTED, but the defendant need only respond if a search for the emails is possible and then, if so, what results were obtained. The contested merits of plaintiff's motion to compel, and defendant's objections thereto are such that no sanctions either way are awardable. Any such requests are DENIED. PLAINTIFF OSCAR ANTONIO MENDEZ ESQUIVEL , AN INDIVIDUAL NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO (PART 4 OF 5)
Off Calendar Jul-19-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2024, line 5, PLAINTIFF OSCAR ANTONIO MENDEZ ESQUIVEL , AN INDIVIDUAL NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO (PART 4 OF 5)

Request No. 9 goes as far back as 2011 on warranty buyback reduction goals. The Court fails to see any connection or relevance between these goals and the facts of this case, especially given the inquiry going back as far as 2011. The motion as to this request is DENIED.
While Request No. 10 was ruled upon above, it exemplifies the perils of imprecise cut and paste efforts. The Court sees unrelated phrases (from a related case?) such as "no audio connection" and "brake light" problems that just hang in the air without connection to this case. Plaintiff's argument in support of its motion also does not match up with Honda's response to the request. That response does not contain any objection about premature expert witness discovery. These textual anomalies simply further support the impression that this litigation is being treated to assembly line, repetitive, cut and paste handling that serves no one's best interests.
Request No. 15 is not overly broad. Limited in scope as discussed above, the definition of STRUCTUAL DEFECTS ("SD") essentially mirrors the plaintiff's defect experiences pretty well. And defendant need only search for instances where other California vehicles had multiple repair opportunities. If such a search is beyond the defendant's abilities, notwithstanding the wizardry of computer search facilities, then defendant can so represent in its amended reply, along the lines of its reply to Request No. 16. The motion to compel is GRANTED.
Request No. 17 seeks NHTSA complaints, and defendant responds only with objections. Are there any such complaints, even close, to 2023 Honda Civics in California? If so, the amended response should identify those documents. The motion to compel is GRANTED.
The response to Request No. 18 recites a willingness to produce TREAD warranty submissions (see Request No. 19), and plaintiff does not indicate that response is evasive or in bad faith. The motion to compel is DENIED. Interesting is the fact Honda's response to No.18 seems unconditioned, covering similar vehicles nationwide. Honda's consistency of objection is a bit undermined by this response.
As for Request No. 19, it is entitled to the same ruling as No. 18 above. (end of part 4 see part 5) = (302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
JOSHUA KOHANOFF, ESQ., QUILL & ARROW, LLP, (310) 933-4271 jkohanoff@quillarrowlaw.com Attorneys for Plaintiff, OSCAR ANTONIO MENDEZ ESQUIVELfor Plaintiff.

Matter is not contested. Plaintiff's counsel appears out of an abundace of caution and stipulates to JPT Scott Borrowman deciding today's motion. Court and counsel authorize the clerk to sign the JPT Stipulation on their behalf.

No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF OSCAR ANTONIO MENDEZ ESQUIVEL Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One

Motion to compel GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. No timely opposition filed. RFP 1, 5, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14 - denied. The response states that all documents will be produced (potentially subject to a protective order). RFP 2, 3, 4, 23, 25, 28 - denied. The response states what will be produced (again, potentially subject to protective order) and the motion does not articulate why these are insufficient. RFP 6, 7, 8, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 24, 26, 27, 29, 31 - denied. The response clearly that that Defendant made a diligent search and has no responsive documents. RFP 30 - granted. The request is limited to a list or compilation and limited to the same make/model as at issue. This is relevant to the potential penalty damages. Sanctions DENIED. As reflected in the fact that most RFPs are denied, Defendant had substantial justification for the responses.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Scott Borrowman at sborrowman@gmail.com.
